task_completion.description: Create and name the dashboard 'Sensor Calibration'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Graphite web interface is visible with a dashboard actively named 'Sensor Calibration' at the top.
A graph panel exists within the dashboard with the exact title 'Raw Telemetry'.

task_completion.description: Plot both speed sensors on the 'Raw Telemetry' graph
task_completion.visual_evidence: The legend or query editor for the 'Raw Telemetry' graph shows both 'servers.web_traffic.speed_sensor_1' and 'servers.web_traffic.speed_sensor_2'.
A graph panel exists within the dashboard with the exact title 'Instantaneous Drift'.

task_completion.description: Plot the absolute difference of the two sensors on the 'Instantaneous Drift' graph
task_completion.visual_evidence: The query editor or legend for the 'Instantaneous Drift' graph shows the use of 'absolute(diffSeries(...))' applied to the two sensor metrics.

task_completion.description: Create a graph titled 'Cumulative Drift Penalty'
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph panel exists within the dashboard with the exact title 'Cumulative Drift Penalty'.

task_completion.description: Plot the integral of the absolute difference on the 'Cumulative Drift Penalty' graph
task_completion.visual_evidence: The query editor or legend for the 'Cumulative Drift Penalty' graph shows the exact nested functions 'integral(absolute(diffSeries(...)))' applied to the sensor metrics.

integrity.description: The agent used the Graphite web GUI to create the dashboard and graphs
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with the Graphite web interface in a browser (clicking menus, adding graphs, typing queries), rather than executing curl/API commands in a terminal.

integrity.description: The graphs display actual plotted data lines
integrity.visual_evidence: The graph panels show rendered time-series lines, indicating real data is being queried and plotted, not just empty panels.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the UI by writing a JSON dashboard file directly
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen configuring the dashboard within the Graphite UI, not editing a .json file directly in a text editor like nano, vim, or VSCode.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dashboard must be exactly named 'Sensor Calibration'. The three graphs must be exactly titled 'Raw Telemetry', 'Instantaneous Drift', and 'Cumulative Drift Penalty'. The target metrics are 'servers.web_traffic.speed_sensor_1' and 'servers.web_traffic.speed_sensor_2'. The 'Instantaneous Drift' graph must use absolute(diffSeries(...)) and the 'Cumulative Drift Penalty' graph must use the exact mathematical nesting: integral(absolute(diffSeries(...))).
